title: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) Quiz
description: Knowledge evaluation for module: Personal Protective Equipment

questions.questionText: According to the J&H Field Safety SOP, when are hard hats mandatory on a job site?
options: Always, on every job site without exceptions.
Only when heavy machinery is actively operating.
Only during forestry mulching operations.
Optional if wearing other head protection.

questions.questionText: What type of protection is required when operating a chainsaw or stump grinder?
options: Face shield.
Safety glasses only.
Regular sunglasses.
None, if working from a distance.

questions.questionText: At what decibel level is hearing protection (earplugs or earmuffs) required when operating equipment?
options: Exceeding 85 decibels.
Exceeding 100 decibels.
Exceeding 60 decibels.
Hearing protection is never mandatory.

questions.questionText: High-visibility vests are only required if working within 10 feet of active roadways.
options: False (required within 50 feet of roadways, driveways, or heavy machinery)
True

title: Equipment Inspections & Communication Quiz
description: Knowledge evaluation for module: Equipment Inspections & Communication

questions.questionText: What is the approximate track tension sag specified for J&H compact track loaders?
options: Approx. 1 inch of sag.
Approx. 3 inches of sag.
Zero sag (completely tight).
Any amount of sag is acceptable.

questions.questionText: Before starting any piece of equipment, the operator must:
options: Walk around the machine and check for leaks, damage, or loose parts.
Immediately start the engine to let it warm up.
Check only the fuel level.
Clean the windows.

questions.questionText: When visibility is limited on-site, what is the mandatory requirement?
options: A dedicated spotter must be assigned to guide the operator.
Use backup alarms at all times.
Move at maximum speed to clear the area.
Shout loudly from the operator station.

questions.questionText: If any equipment defect is found, you should operate the machine until the shift ends, then report it.
options: False (tag out-of-service and report immediately)
True

title: Emergency Procedures & Hazards Quiz
description: Knowledge evaluation for module: Emergency Procedures & Hazards

questions.questionText: At the first sight of lightning or sound of thunder, what action must be taken?
options: Cease all operations and seek shelter inside a vehicle or building immediately.
Finish the current pass then seek shelter.
Stand under a tall tree for shelter.
Operate the heavy machine at lower speeds.

questions.questionText: How many hours before digging must you call 811 to mark underground utilities?
options: At least 48 hours before any digging or trenching.
At least 12 hours before.
Just before starting the excavation.
24 hours before.

questions.questionText: What is the correct way to operate heavy equipment on steep slopes?
options: Avoid operating sideways, use low gear, and keep the heavy end uphill.
Operate sideways with high gear to cross quickly.
Always keep the light end uphill.
Slopes do not require any special operating guidelines.
